sections.title: Executive Summary
sections.takeaway: Reduce effective cycle time at Drivetrain Assembly with standard work, faster changeovers, or error removal before spending on larger structural changes.

sections.title: System Diagnosis
sections.takeaway: Drivetrain Assembly is taking demand at about 13.5 units/hr but only clearing about 3.2 units/hr, so arrivals are outrunning service and the queue cannot normalize.

actions.title: Stabilize the active constraint first
actions.why: Reduce effective cycle time at Drivetrain Assembly with standard work, faster changeovers, or error removal before spending on larger structural changes.
actions.effect: Expected immediate gain is about 0.32 units/hr if the bottleneck mechanism is removed.

actions.title: Run the first improvement event at Frame Kit Prep
actions.why: Changeovers, batching, and weak release rules add non-value time before units clear Frame Kit Prep. That inflates queue delay to 2644.3 min, stretches lead time to 2648.3 min, and Frame Kit Prep feeds instability into Drivetrain Assembly, which keeps the system bottleneck from recovering.
actions.effect: Likely leverage: 559.4 min less queue delay and 336.2 min less lead time.

actions.title: Put release and WIP discipline around the largest queue
actions.why: Frame Kit Prep is carrying the heaviest queue and is driving system-level delay.
actions.effect: Reduces hidden delay and prevents the line from flooding the next near-constraint.

actions.title: Separate data validation from action execution
actions.why: Confirm this assumption with the process owner before treating results as final.
actions.effect: Improves trust in the recommendation without delaying immediate containment work.

actions.title: Attack structural waiting at Frame Kit Prep
actions.why: Frame Kit Prep is contributing the largest delay pocket in the current modeled path.
actions.effect: Separates touch time from waiting so the team works on real delay instead of anecdotal friction.
